name: Generative AI
desc: LLMs in production — tokenize, sample, embed, retrieve, and force structured tool calls.

questions.card: from_pretrained fetches the matching tokenizer.
questions.mission: Load the gpt2 tokenizer into tok.

questions.card: Models read token ids; return_tensors="pt" = torch.
questions.mission: Encode prompt to torch tensors into ids.

questions.card: Causal LMs predict the next token.
questions.mission: Load gpt2 as a causal LM into model.

questions.card: max_new_tokens caps the output length.
questions.mission: Generate up to 50 new tokens from ids into out.

questions.card: decode turns ids back into text.
questions.mission: Decode the first sequence of out into text.

questions.card: Decode a whole batch at once.
questions.mission: Decode every sequence in out into texts.

questions.card: Ship the model to the accelerator.
pipeline wraps tokenizer + model for a task.
Decoder-only models often reuse EOS as PAD.
len(tokenizer) is the vocab size.
questions.mission: Store the vocab size in vocab.

questions.card: do_sample=True + temperature softens the distribution.
questions.mission: Sample from ids at temperature 0.7 into out.

questions.card: Keep only the smallest set of tokens summing to p.
Restrict to the k most likely tokens.
do_sample=False always takes the argmax.
Track several hypotheses in parallel.
repetition_penalty discourages loops.
questions.mission: Generate with a repetition penalty of 1.2 into out.

questions.card: Fix the RNG so sampling repeats.
Pass an explicit end-of-sequence id.
Bundle decoding params in a GenerationConfig.
questions.mission: Config with temperature 0.7, top_p 0.9 into cfg.

questions.card: Stream tokens as they are produced.
Sentence models project text into a shared vector space.
.encode maps texts to vectors.
Unit vectors make cosine = dot product.
The question goes through the same encoder.
cosine_similarity scores query vs corpus.
questions.mission: Similarities of q_vec to vecs into sims.

questions.card: sentence-transformers ships util.cos_sim.
The second axis is the vector width.
questions.mission: Read the embedding dim of vecs into dim.

questions.card: The most similar chunk is the argmax.
questions.mission: Index of the best match in sims into best.

questions.card: Sort and slice the tail for the k best.
questions.mission: Indices of the 3 best in sims into top.

questions.card: For normalized vectors, cosine is just the dot.
questions.mission: Dot a with b into score.

questions.card: Chroma stores embeddings + metadata.
questions.mission: Create a Chroma client into client.

questions.card: Collections group related vectors.
questions.mission: Create a docs collection into collection.

questions.card: Chroma embeds text for you on add.
questions.mission: Add docs with ids to the collection.

questions.card: Query returns the nearest chunks.
questions.mission: Retrieve the 4 nearest for q into hits.

questions.card: where restricts by metadata.
questions.mission: Query with a filter {"year": 2026} into hits.

questions.card: FAISS is a fast in-memory ANN index.
questions.mission: Flat L2 index over 384-d vectors into index.

questions.card: Populate the index with your embeddings.
Search returns distances and indices.
questions.mission: Search the 4 nearest to q_vec into D, I.

questions.card: Split long docs into fixed windows before embedding.
questions.mission: Chunk text into 500-char slices into chunks.
questions.ph: chunks = [text[i:i+500] for i in range(0, len(text), 500)]
questions.sol: chunks = [text[i:i+500] for i in range(0, len(text), 500)]

questions.card: Join retrieved chunks into the prompt context.
questions.mission: Join hits with spaces into context.

questions.card: Describe a tool the model can call.
questions.mission: Define tool with a name and parameters.

questions.card: tool_choice="required" forces structured output.
questions.mission: Call the model with msgs + tools, forcing a call, into resp.

questions.card: The call rides on the response message.
questions.mission: Pull the first tool call from resp into call.

questions.card: Tool args arrive as a JSON string.
Map tool names to the real functions.
questions.mission: Map "get_weather" to get_weather into registry.

questions.card: Look up the function and spread the args.
questions.mission: Call registry[name] with **args into result.

questions.card: Feed the tool output back as a message.
questions.mission: Append a tool message with result to messages.

questions.card: Prepend the agent's instructions.
questions.mission: Append a system message with prompt to messages.

questions.card: LangChain attaches tools to the LLM.
questions.mission: Bind tools to llm into agent.

questions.card: Run the reasoning loop over the messages.
questions.mission: Invoke agent on messages into reply.

questions.card: The system message sets the assistant's persona / constraints.
questions.mission: Set role system, content Be concise..

questions.card: Give input→output pairs so the model imitates the pattern.
Ask the model to think step by step; often improves accuracy.
questions.mission: Set instruction: Think step by step..

questions.card: Reason + act loops: Thought / Action / Observation.
questions.mission: Start line: Thought: I need to search..

questions.card: Sample N reasoning paths, pick majority answer.
stop aborts generation when hit.
Constrain outputs with JSON schema / pydantic.
questions.mission: Declare class header for Answer extending BaseModel.

questions.card: temperature=0 — deterministic decoding.
str.format injects variables into a prompt template.
questions.mission: Fill tpl with topic='python' into p.

questions.card: Never pipe raw user text into a system prompt.
LoraConfig — the PEFT recipe.
get_peft_model(model, cfg) wraps the base model.
BitsAndBytesConfig(load_in_4bit=True) shrinks memory.
TrainingArguments — HF Trainer config.
SFTTrainer — supervised fine-tuning helper.
Kick off training.
Publish weights to the Hugging Face Hub.
Perplexity = e^loss.
DPOTrainer aligns with preference pairs.
merge_and_unload folds LoRA into base weights.
sacrebleu — translation quality (n-gram overlap).
rouge_scorer — summarization overlap.
Ask an LLM to grade another LLM's output on a rubric.
ragas scores RAG on faithfulness/answer_relevancy.
Strip emails etc before storing/logging.
pydantic validates model output on parse.
Detect refusal via prefix match.
Cap generation to bound cost/latency.
Track tokens × price per 1K.
Simple check for adversarial prompt patterns.
FastAPI() — declare an app.
Decorate a handler with @app.post.
StreamingResponse — token-by-token.
uvicorn — ASGI server.
Cache prompt→response.
langfuse.trace logs an LLM span.
vLLM batches many prompts on one GPU.
FROM starts the base image.
InferenceClient — hosted inference.
Simple /health route for k8s liveness probes.
